audit_status.source_base: Greek James text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for favoritism, poor/rich reversal, royal law, mercy over judgment, trust and works, Abraham/Rahab examples.

verses.restored: My siblings, do not hold the trust of our Lord Jesus Messiah of glory with favoritism.
For if a gold-ringed man in shining clothing enters your gathering, and a poor one in dirty clothing also enters,
and you look upon the one wearing shining clothing and say, You sit here beautifully, and to the poor one you say, You stand there or Sit under my footstool,
have you not made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with harmful reasonings?
Listen, my beloved siblings: did not the Divine choose the poor in the world to be rich in trust and heirs of the reign that he promised to those loving him?
But you dishonored the poor. Do not the rich oppress you and drag you into courts?
Do they not blaspheme the beautiful/good name called over you?
If indeed you fulfill royal Law according to the Scripture, You shall love your neighbor as yourself, you do beautifully/well.
But if you show favoritism, you work sin/misalignment, being exposed by the Law as transgressors.
For whoever keeps the whole Law but stumbles in one thing has become accountable for all.
For the One who said, Do not commit adultery, also said, Do not murder. If you do not commit adultery but murder, you have become transgressor of Law.
So speak and so do as those about to be judged through Law of freedom.
For judgment is without mercy to the one not doing mercy; mercy boasts over judgment.
What benefit, my siblings, if someone says one has trust but does not have works? Is the trust able to save/heal that one?
If a sibling is naked and lacking daily food,
and one of you says to them, Go in peace, be warmed and filled, but you do not give the things needful for the body, what benefit?
So also trust, if it does not have works, is dead by itself.
But someone will say, You have trust, and I have works. Show me your trust apart from works, and I will show you trust by my works.
You trust that Divine is one; you do beautifully. Even the daimons trust and shudder.
But do you want to know, empty human, that trust apart from works is inactive?
Was not Abraham our father made righteous/just by works, offering Isaac his son upon the altar?
You see that trust was working together with his works, and by the works the trust was completed.
And the Scripture was fulfilled saying, Abraham trusted the Divine, and it was counted to him as righteousness/justice, and he was called friend of the Divine.
You see that a human is made righteous/just by works and not by trust alone.
And likewise also Rahab the sex worker, was she not made righteous/just by works, having welcomed the messengers and sent them out by another way?
For just as the body apart from spirit/breath is dead, so also trust apart from works is dead.

translation_notes: James 2 is anti-favoritism and materially concrete: bodily need cannot be answered by words alone.
Trust and works are not abstract theological opponents here; living trust is completed in embodied action.
Rahab is named without sanitizing her status; her agency and hospitality are honored.
